title: piper lappaceum
intro: Piper lappaceum, also known as Long pepper, is a plant native to India and Southeast Asia, belonging to the Piperaceae family. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Piper lappaceum include reducing fever, relieving pain, and aiding digestion, as well as exhibiting potential anti-cancer and anti-diabetic properties. The plant is commonly used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and decoctions, and can also be consumed as a spice or used in traditional Ayurvedic and Unani medicine.
